scene_input.scene_summary: Abū Bakr hears the verse that changes his heart
scene_input.key_visual_action: Abū Bakr looking surprised and thoughtful

schema_output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, p. 211
schema_output.moral_lesson: The power of faith and its impact on one's life

characters.name: Abū Bakr
characters.appearance: tall, slender build, dignified posture, wearing a long white thobe with a plain brown woolen cloak and a white cloth head covering

schema_output.narrative_text: Abū Bakr was a respected leader in Makkah, but when he heard the verse, his heart began to open to the message of Islam.
schema_output.image_prompt: Children's watercolor storybook illustration, soft warm hand-painted style for ages 6-12. A quiet, contemplative space with simple, unadorned walls and a low wooden bench in the center of the room. Late afternoon sunlight streams through a small window on the right, casting a warm glow. A lone figure sits on the bench, seen from behind: tall, slender build, dignified posture, wearing a long white thobe with a plain brown woolen cloak and a white cloth head covering. His head is slightly tilted forward, and his hands are clasped together in a thoughtful gesture. Soft warm golden light, intimate and contemplative mood. Medium close shot at eye level. Warm beige, cream, and golden palette with the plain brown cloak as a color anchor. No modern objects, historically accurate 7th century scene.
_safety_changes: 'lone figure' could be considered ambiguous, but no change made for now
